mnemonics.title: Cloud Service Models
mnemonics.content: SIPS: SaaS (Software), IaaS (Infrastructure), PaaS (Platform) - Think 'SIP' while choosing cloud service level

mnemonics.title: Hypervisor Types
mnemonics.content: Type-1 = 'True' (bare-metal, runs directly); Type-2 = 'Toy' (hosted, runs on OS) - Remember T1 is Faster and T2 is Tacked on

mnemonics.title: Virtualization Benefits
mnemonics.content: SAVE: Scalability, Availability, Utilization, Efficiency - Virtualization 'SAVES' resources

mnemonics.title: Cloud Deployment Models
mnemonics.content: PUB-PVT-HYBRID: Public cost-effective, Private secure, Hybrid flexible - Think 'PPH' for Planning

acronyms.usage: Emulated computer system running on physical hardware
Another term for hypervisor managing VMs
Cloud model providing virtualized computing resources
Cloud model providing development and deployment platform
Cloud model delivering software applications over internet

acronyms.expansion: National Institute of Standards and Technology
acronyms.usage: US agency defining cloud computing standards
Hosting desktop environments on central servers
Virtualizing network services like firewalls, routers

visualTips.visualization: Draw a triangle: Physical Hardware (bottom) → Hypervisor (middle) → Multiple VMs (top) - like layers of a cake
Pyramid: SaaS at top (user sees only apps), PaaS in middle (user sees platform), IaaS at bottom (user controls infrastructure) - like descending into more control
Type-1: Box on floor (direct); Type-2: Box on table with blanket (OS in between) - Remember bare-metal touches floor directly

analogies.analogy: Like an apartment building - one physical building (hardware) divided into multiple separate living spaces (VMs) with independent facilities
Container is like a shipping container (lightweight, standardized), VM is like a full house (complete with all facilities) - containers share common infrastructure like containers share ships

analogies.analization: Like a rubber band - stretches (scales up) during high demand and shrinks (scales down) when demand decreases

analogies.analogy: Like a building manager - manages and allocates resources among different tenants without them knowing about each other
